perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall (i) use reasonable efforts to maintain the confidentiality of the information and materials, whether oral, written or in any form whatsoever, of the other that may be reasonably understood, from legend s, the nature of such information itself and/or the circumstances of such information’s disclosure, to be confidential and/or proprietary thereto or to third parties to which either of them owes a duty of nondisclosure (collectively, “Confidential Informat ion”); (ii) take reasonable action in connection therewith, including without limitation at least the action that each takes to protect the confidentiality of its comparable proprietary assets; (iii) to the extent within their respective possession and/or control, upon termination of this Agreement for any reason, immediately return to the provider thereof all Confidential Information not licensed or authorized to be used or enjoyed after termination or expiration hereof, and (iv) with respect to any perso n to which disclosure is contemplated, require such person to execute an agreement providing for the treatment of Confidential Information set forth in clauses (i) through (iii).
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall (i) use reasonable efforts to maintain the confidentiality of the information and materials, whether oral, written or in any form whatsoever, of the other that may be reasonably understood, from legend s, the nature of such information itself and/or the circumstances of such information’s disclosure, to be protected information and/or proprietary thereto or to third parties to which either of them owes a duty of nondisclosure (collectively, “Confidential Informat ion”); (ii) take reasonable action in connection therewith, including without limitation at least the action that each takes to protect the confidentiality of its comparable proprietary assets; (iii) to the extent within their respective possession and/or control, upon termination of this Agreement for any reason, immediately return to the provider thereof all Confidential Information not licensed or authorized to be used or enjoyed after termination or expiration hereof, and (iv) with respect to any perso n to which disclosure is contemplated, require such person to execute an agreement providing for the treatment of Confidential Information set forth in clauses (i) through (iii).
perturbation.explanation: Substituting 'confidential' with 'protected' creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement defines 'Confidential Information' based on its 'confidential' nature. By using 'protected information' without explicitly including it in the definition of 'Confidential Information', it's unclear if these two are the same, thereby rendering Section 3 on Confidential Information ambiguous. This creates an uncertain term with 'protected information', which can lead to conflicts and issues of interpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information delivered pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party, and any documents containing or reflecting the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to the disclosing Party upon written request, or destroyed at the disclosing Party’s option. Nothing herein shall be construed as granting or conferr ing any rights by license or otherwise, express or implied, regarding any idea made, conceived or acquired prior to or after the Effective Date, nor as granting any right with respect to the use or marketing of any product or service. The Parties shall us e the Confidential Information only for the Business Relationship. 2 The obligations of the Parties under this Agreement shall continue and survive the completion or abandonment of the Business Relationship and shall remain binding for a period of two (2) y ears from the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'confidential' with 'protected' in the definition of 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text explicitly refers to 'Confidential Information'. The change creates ambiguity as it is not certain whether 'protected information' falls under the umbrella of 'Confidential Information', making it unclear whether the contradicted text still applies, which is a clear contradiction as per Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information delivered pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party, and any documents containing or reflecting the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to the disclosing Party upon written request, or destroyed at the disclosing Party’s option. Nothing herein shall be construed as granting or conferr ing any rights by license or otherwise, express or implied, regarding any idea made, conceived or acquired prior to or after the Effective Date, nor as granting any right with respect to the use or marketing of any product or service. The Parties shall us e the Confidential Information only for the Business Relationship. 2 The obligations of the Parties under this Agreement shall continue and survive the completion or abandonment of the Business Relationship and shall remain binding for a period of two (2) y ears from the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information delivered pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party, and any documents containing or reflecting the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to the disclosing Party upon written request, or destroyed at the disclosing Party’s option. Nothing herein shall be construed as granting or conferr ing any rights by license or otherwise, express or implied, regarding any idea made, conceived or acquired prior to or after the Effective Date, nor as granting any right with respect to the use or marketing of any product or service. The Parties shall us e the Confidential Information only for the Partnership. 2 The obligations of the Parties under this Agreement shall continue and survive the completion or abandonment of the Business Relationship and shall remain binding for a period of two (2) y ears from the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Business Relationship' to 'Partnership' creates an in-text contradiction. The agreement is framed around a 'Business Relationship' and changing it mid-section leads to uncertainty about the scope and application of the agreement, potentially invalidating Section 3. The rest of the document consistently refers to the 'Business Relationship,' so a change here without corresponding updates elsewhere causes confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and is intended as the Parties’ final expression and complete and exclusive statement of the terms thereof, superseding all prior or contemporaneous agreem ents, representations, promises and understandings, whether written or oral. Neither Party is to be bound by any pre -printed terms appearing in the other Party’s form documents, tariffs, purchase orders, quotations, acknowledgments, invoices, or other ins truments. This Agreement may be amended or modified only by an instrument in writing signed by both Parties.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Business Relationship' to 'Partnership' in the Changed Text introduces a contradiction with the Contradicted Text. The Contradicted Text emphasizes that the agreement constitutes the 'entire agreement and understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof.' By changing 'Business Relationship' to 'Partnership' in one section while the rest of the document, including the Contradicted Text, refers to 'Business Relationship,' the agreement now has inconsistent terminology regarding its scope and subject matter. This creates ambiguity and undermines the claim in the contradicted text that it is the 'complete and exclusive statement of the terms thereof,' thereby creating an in-text contradiction as the parties may be in disagreement over which term defines their obligations. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: As a violation by either Party of this Agreement could cause irreparable injury to the other Party and as there is no adequate remedy at law for such violation, the non -breaching Party may, in additi on to any other remedies available to it at law or in equity, enjoin the breaching Party in a court of equity for violating or threatening to violate this Agreement. In the event either Party is required to enforce this Agreement through legal action, the n it will be entitled to recover from the other Party all costs incurred thereby, including without limitation, reasonable attorney’s fees.
perturbation.changed_text: As a violation by either Party of this Agreement could cause irreparable injury to the other Party and as there is no adequate remedy at law for such violation, the non -breaching Party may, in additi on to any other remedies available to it at law or in equity, enjoin the violating Party in a court of equity for violating or threatening to violate this Agreement. In the event either Party is required to enforce this Agreement through legal action, the n it will be entitled to recover from the other Party all costs incurred thereby, including without limitation, reasonable attorney’s fees.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'breaching Party' with 'violating Party' creates subtle confusion in Section 4. While seemingly synonymous, 'breaching Party' is the established legal term within the document. The substitution implies that a 'violating Party' might encompass actions beyond a formal breach, potentially expanding the scope of actions subject to injunction, and creates doubt about what constitutes a violation of the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a violation by either Party of this Agreement could cause irreparable injury to the other Party and as there is no adequate remedy at law for such violation, the non -breaching Party may, in additi on to any other remedies available to it at law or in equity, enjoin the violating Party in a court of equity for violating or threatening to violate this Agreement. In the event either Party is required to enforce this Agreement through legal action, the n it will be entitled to recover from the other Party all costs incurred thereby, including without limitation, reasonable attorney’s fees.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'breaching Party' with 'violating Party'. According to the Perturbation Explanation, this creates subtle confusion because 'breaching Party' is the established legal term within the document, and the substitution implies that a 'violating Party' might encompass actions beyond a formal breach. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology, creating an in-text contradiction. The Changed Text and Contradicted Text occur in the same place. The modified text's shift in terminology introduces ambiguity, potentially broadening the scope of actions subject to injunction, thereby contradicting the established and potentially narrower interpretation of 'breaching Party' elsewhere in the agreement.
